title: Extraction - Optimization - AI Translation - Reinsertion
platform_tooltip: This platform is under development and will be enabled soon
manual_step_1_content:
🎯 What It Does

Copies all text from your game (dialogues, menus, items) into a file you can read.

📝 How to Use

  1. 1. Choose Console: Click where it says platform (example: Super Nintendo, PlayStation 1, Game Boy Advance, PC Games)
  2. 2. Choose Game: Click "Select ROM" and pick your game file
  3. 3. Press Button: Click "Extract Texts"
  4. 4. Wait to Finish: The green bar will fill up to 100%

✅ What Will Happen

  • A text file will be created named: game_name_extracted.txt
  • Takes 30 seconds to 2 minutes
  • You can see progress in the green bar

⚠️ Tips

  • If game is in Japanese, you'll see strange symbols (this is normal!)
  • Some games may have little text - don't worry, continue to next step
manual_step_2_content:
🎯 What It Does

Cleans the text file by removing repeated things and nonsense text. This makes the file much smaller!

📝 How to Use

  1. 1. Press Button: Click "Optimize Data" (still in Extraction tab)
  2. 2. Wait to Clean: Program will clean automatically
  3. 3. See Result: At the bottom it will show how much it reduced

✅ What Will Happen

  • New file created: game_name_optimized.txt
  • Real example: From 755,000 lines to 150,000 lines (saves 80%!)
  • Takes only 5 to 15 seconds

💡 Why Do This?

  • Save Money: Less text = you pay less for translation
  • Faster: Translation becomes 5 times faster
  • Better Quality: Removes nonsense text that messes up translation

🧹 What Gets Removed

  • Repeated text (if "Hello" appears 100 times, keeps only 1)
  • Random nonsense letters (like "XYZABC", "QQWWRR")
  • Weird symbols and game codes
manual_step_3_content:
🎯 What It Does

Translates game text with AI using automatic or manual flow.

🚀 Recommended Flow (Automatic)

  1. 1. Select ROM using ROM/Game File
  2. 2. Click "🚀 Translate All (Auto)"
  3. 3. Wait for 3 stages: Extraction -> Translation -> Reinsertion
  4. 4. Final ROM: GameName_TRANSLATING_PT-BR.ext

🧠 Translation Modes (Translation tab)

  • 🤖 AUTO (Gemini + NLLB): tries Gemini first, then falls back to offline NLLB-200
  • ⚡ Gemini (Google AI): online, higher quality
  • 🔤 NLLB-200 (Offline): local, no internet required

🔑 API Key

Gemini API key is configured in the Settings tab. Without a key, the app uses offline NLLB automatically.

💡 Tip

If you want full control, use the manual flow in Extraction, Translation and Reinsertion tabs.
manual_step_4_content:
🎯 What It Does

Puts translated text back inside the game. This is the final step!

📝 HOW TO USE (Step-by-Step Visual Guide)

🔹 STEP 1: Go to Reinsertion Tab

At the top of the window, click the "📥 3. Reinsertion" tab

🔹 STEP 2: Choose Original Game

• Click "📂 Original ROM" then "Select ROM"
• Choose the same game file you used at the beginning (.smc, .bin, .nds, etc.)

🔹 STEP 3: Choose Translated Text

• Click "📄 Translated File" then "Select File"
• Choose the *_translated.txt file created in Step 3

🔹 STEP 4: Name Your Translated Game

• In the "💾 Translated ROM (Output)" field
• Type a name, example: MyGame_EN.smc

🔹 STEP 5: Create Translated Game

• Press the "Reinsert Translation" button
• Wait 10 to 60 seconds
• Done! Your translated game is created!

✅ What Happened?

  • ✅ New game created with the name you chose
  • ✅ Your original game stays intact (not modified)
  • ✅ Now just test it in the emulator!

🎮 How to Test Translated Game

🔹 What is An Emulator?

It's a program that lets you play old console games on your computer.

🔹 Recommended Emulators (Download Free)

  • Super Nintendo (SNES): SNES9x or ZSNES
  • PlayStation 1: ePSXe or DuckStation
  • Game Boy Advance: VisualBoy Advance
  • Nintendo DS: DeSmuME or melonDS

🔹 How to Test (Simple!)

  1. Open the emulator for the correct console
  2. Click File → Open
  3. Choose your translated game
  4. Play a bit and see if text is in your language!

💡 Important Tips

  • ✅ Always test translated game before sharing
  • ✅ Use emulator Save States to save anytime
  • ✅ Keep the *_translated.txt file - you may need it later
  • ✅ If you get an error, you can redo just Step 4 (it's fast!)
  • ✅ Share your work with other players! 🎮

🎉 Congratulations! You Did It!

🎊 YOUR GAME IS READY!

✅ Translation complete!
✅ Game created successfully!
✅ Everything working perfectly!

Now just play and have fun! 🎮✨

❓ Frequently Asked Questions (If You Have Any Doubts)

🔽 Click here if you encounter any problems (rare, but can happen)

🔸 Some Text Appears Cut Off

Why it happens: Translations are naturally longer than Japanese/English.

Solution: This is common in translations! You can:

  • ✅ Play normally - you can understand even when cut off
  • 🔧 If you know how to edit text: shorten sentences in *_translated.txt and redo reinsertion

🔸 Weird Symbols Instead of Accents

Why it happens: The original game wasn't programmed for special letters (ã, ñ, é, etc.)

Solution: You can:

  • ✅ Play normally - "cafe" is easy to understand!
  • 🔧 If you know how to edit text: replace accents with normal letters in *_translated.txt

🔸 Game Won't Open in Emulator

What to do:

  1. Try using a different emulator (each works better with certain games)
  2. Check if you chose the right emulator for the console
  3. If it doesn't work, download the original game again and redo only Step 4

🔧 For Advanced Users - Manual Editing

How to edit the translation file:

  1. Right-click *_translated.txt
  2. Choose "Open with... → Notepad"
  3. Edit whatever you want (shorten sentences, replace accents, fix errors)
  4. Save (Ctrl+S)
  5. Go back to the program and redo only Step 4 (Reinsertion)

developer_footer: Developed by Celso - Solo Programmer | © 2026 All rights reserved
welcome_body:
Welcome to NEUROROM.

Recommended flow:
1) Click ROM/Game File and select your ROM
2) Click 🚀 Translate All (Auto)
3) Wait: Extraction -> Translation -> Reinsertion
4) The final translated ROM will be generated automatically

Optional: in Developer mode, you can run the tabs manually.
